label: Mid-1990s America
description: Clinton era prosperity. Internet just arriving. SUV boom beginning. Grunge and hip-hop culture. Big box retail killing strip malls.

specific_models: 1992-1997 Ford Taurus — rounded jellybean shape, oval theme
1995-1999 Dodge Neon — small rounded economy, friendly face
1993-2002 Pontiac Firebird — sleek pointed sports coupe
1991-1994 Saturn SL — plastic body panels, compact
1993-1998 Jeep Grand Cherokee — early SUV, boxy-rounded
1991-1996 Buick Roadmaster — large rounded wagon and sedan
visual_characteristics: plastic body cladding on lower panels
multi-spoke alloy wheels on nicer models

specific_models: 1994-2001 Dodge Ram — big rig style grille, bold

fuel.context: Stable low prices, SUV boom accelerating fuel consumption

characteristics: credit card readers at pump beginning
early LED price signs at gas stations
signage.typography_style: clean geometric sans-serif, early digital influence

characteristics: more traffic signals with left turn phases
no variable message signs yet common
green highway signs with white lettering

items: pay phones still common — standalone and wall mounted

characteristics: cable TV and early phone data lines on poles

advertising.style: photographic, clean layouts, early desktop publishing aesthetic

characteristics: manicured landscaping around big box stores

lighting.street_lights: high pressure sodium vapor — orange glow dominant
lighting.commercial: fluorescent lighting standard, early compact fluorescent appearing
lighting.signs: backlit plastic and neon, early LED beginning in price signs

photography.style: snapshot documentary, natural daylight, consumer point-and-shoot feel

downtown_street.narrative: A 1995 main street — the video rental store at its absolute peak, a computer shop newly arrived, chain logos displacing the last hand-painted signs.
storefronts: video rental store at its peak with a big backlit 'VIDEO RENTALS — NEW RELEASES' sign and a 'BE KIND, REWIND' sticker on the door
pizza and sub shop with a '2 SLICES + SODA $2.99' sign in the window
{DINER_NAME} with 'HOT COFFEE 75¢' in the window
computer shop with a 'PENTIUM PCs — INTERNET SOFTWARE' banner
barber shop with its spinning barber pole, unchanged
music store with a 'CDs — CASSETTES' channel-letter sign
pager and cellular store with a neon 'PAGERS — BEEPERS' sign
coffee-and-bagel cafe with a chalkboard sandwich board
people_activities: a teenager in a flannel shirt browsing the video store's new releases wall, visible through the glass
a man in baggy jeans carrying a stack of rented VHS tapes
a woman talking on the wall-mounted payphone
a group of teens in grunge flannel outside the music store
a businessman talking on a large early cell phone
a mother pushing a stroller past the diner
a clerk taping a sale poster inside a shop window
two kids on a bench trading collectible cards
a man walking a dog past the computer shop
extras: a wall-mounted payphone or payphone booth — REQUIRED, must be clearly visible
the old painted ghost-sign now almost fully faded, barely legible
movie release posters plastered in the video store windows
a cluster of coin-operated newspaper boxes on the corner

gas_station.narrative: A 1995 self-serve station — wide canopy over multiple islands, attached convenience store, the first pay-at-pump card readers.
storefronts: wide flat canopy spanning multiple pump islands with a brand color band
road pole sign with early-LED price digits
self-serve pumps, some fitted with new credit-card readers
attached convenience store with lottery and cigarette ads in the glass
ice merchandiser and stacked firewood bundles outside the store
automatic car wash bay at the back of the lot
air and water station at the lot edge
people_activities: a driver self-serving at the pump
a woman swiping a card at the new pay-at-pump reader
a man walking out of the store with a soda and a bag of chips
a clerk restocking the ice merchandiser
a driver checking tire pressure at the air station
a parent and kid walking into the store
extras: squeegee buckets at the pump islands
a hand-written 'PUMP 3 OUT OF ORDER' sign taped to one pump
stacked cases of soda on a pallet outside the store

strip_mall.narrative: A 1995 strip mall past its prime but still busy — big backlit box signs, a video store anchor, the first vacant units appearing.
storefronts: video rental anchor at its peak with a huge backlit 'VIDEO — NEW RELEASES' sign
Chinese takeout with a 'LUNCH SPECIAL $3.95' menu taped to the glass
dollar store with a yellow 'EVERYTHING 99¢' banner
used video game store with a 'WE BUY & SELL GAMES' sign
pager and cellular store with a neon 'PAGERS' sign
nail salon with a neon 'NAILS' sign
tax and insurance office with vertical blinds in the window
one vacant unit with soaped windows and a 'FOR LEASE' sign
people_activities: a family loading takeout bags into a minivan
teenagers comparing game cartridges through the store window
a woman pushing a cart of dollar-store bags
a man carrying a stack of rented videotapes to his car
two teens skateboarding along the curb
a woman leaving the nail salon waving her nails dry
a clerk on a break by the service door
extras: a wall-mounted payphone bank near the anchor store
a cart corral with a few stray carts
a grand-opening banner strung across the dollar store

default.narrative: An ordinary 1995 American commercial street — backlit box signs, rounded cars, the video store era at full strength.
storefronts: {DINER_NAME} with 'HOT COFFEE 75¢' in the window
barber shop with its spinning barber pole
video rental store with a backlit 'VIDEO RENTALS' sign and a 'BE KIND, REWIND' sticker
computer shop with a 'PENTIUM PCs' window banner
music store with a 'CDs — CASSETTES' sign
pizza and sub shop with combo deal posters
people_activities: a teenager in flannel carrying rented VHS tapes
a woman talking on a wall-mounted payphone
a mother pushing a stroller
a man walking a dog
a clerk taping a poster inside a window
two kids on a bench trading cards
a businessman with a large early cell phone
extras: the old ghost-sign almost fully faded
